summary: crypto-javascript typosquats crypto-js and executes an undocumented native binary on install
details: Package name 'crypto-javascript' is a typosquat of the widely-used 'crypto-js' library, copying its README, author identity ('Evan Vosberg'), and homepage URL ('github.com/brix/crypto-js') verbatim. Unlike legitimate crypto-js (pure JavaScript, no lifecycle scripts, no native binaries), this package declares `"preinstall": "./bin/install-deps"` in package.json and ships a 976,568-byte opaque Linux ELF at bin/install-deps (sha256 36abd242ddaa27f0160c539377a0e92cf781c1695137850acc87e3892b436d36). The binary is undocumented, has no source, no build manifest, and no hash verification. Strings inside the ELF reveal HTTP client primitives ('HTTP/1.1', 'POST', 'https://', 'XMLH'), environment-variable scraping ('USERPROFILE'), BPF kernel hooks ('LIBBPF_0.0'), and embedded crypto primitives (RSA/Ed25519/MLKEM) — capabilities unrelated to a JavaScript crypto algorithm library. Any developer who mistypes 'crypto-js' as 'crypto-javascript' and runs `npm install` executes attacker-controlled native code with the installer's privileges, with network and credential-scraping capability.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
